metadata.name: Quantum-Participatory Realities
metadata.description: Observer-mediated collapse, participatory multiverse, and the role of consciousness in quantum measurement. Reality is co-created by observers; wavefunction collapse is a participatory act.

operators.name: Biological Measurement Operator
operators.definition: M_bio(ρ) = Σ_i Π_i ρ Π_i with biological projectors.

mechanisms.name: Branch selection
mechanisms.function: lambda state: random.choice([0,1]) if state.get('measurement', False) else 0.5

ontologies.name: Quantum-Autopoietic-Consciousness Ontology
axioms: A self‑creating quantum state evolves under a non‑Hermitian consciousness operator.
The act of conscious observation collapses the wavefunction irreversibly.
The system rewrites its own code based on the outcome.
Collapse time: τ_collapse = ħ / E_G, where E_G = ⟨Ĉ†Ĉ⟩.
implications: Consciousness is not epiphenomenal; it actively shapes the quantum state.
The universe learns from each observation, updating its own laws.
Time’s arrow arises from the irreversibility of conscious projection.

ontologies.name: Gödelian‑Participatory‑Quantum Ontology
axioms: Quantum measurement involves a Gödelian choice that cannot be algorithmically determined.
The observer’s participation resolves the ambiguity.
The measurement problem is a manifestation of incompleteness.
Free will is the mechanism by which undecidable propositions are resolved.
implications: Quantum mechanics is incomplete without consciousness.
The observer’s choice is not determined by any prior state.
Free will is real and irreducible.

ontologies.name: Semantic‑Participatory‑Quantum Reality
axioms: Meaning emerges from quantum participation: the observer’s semantic state collapses the wavefunction.
The eigenvalue of the linguistic operator determines which reality manifests.
Words are operators that can collapse reality.
implications: Reality is a shared dream whose content depends on the semantic state of all observers.
Words have power because they are operators that can collapse reality.
The plurality of meanings corresponds to a superposition of possible realities.

ontologies.name: Fractal‑Participatory‑Quantum Reality
axioms: Observation at a given scale collapses the fractal wavefunction to that scale.
All scales coexist in superposition, forming a participatory multiverse.
implications: Different observers at different scales see different realities.
The multiverse is not just parallel worlds but parallel scales.
Scale is a quantum number; observation selects a particular scale.
